_provenance: Fully SYNTHETIC and GENERIC hybrid-dispatch routing policy authored for this evaluation. It encodes the documented hard-rules contract of the hybrid-dispatch router (frameworks/openweights-finetuning/internals/hybrid-dispatch) extended with a third 'edge' tier. It is NOT any real production routing table, model roster, cost sheet, or capability matrix. Difficulty thresholds, per-tier capability sets, and cost weights are illustrative constructs for exercising eval metrics, not operational thresholds for any real router.
_schema: tiers: ordered cheapest->priciest (edge, local, frontier). Each tier has id, capabilities (the set it can satisfy), max_difficulty (the inclusive upper difficulty it will accept under the difficulty rule; frontier accepts everything), cost (relative weight for the tiebreak). 'net' marks whether the tier leaves the box: edge/local = none, frontier = frontier. A privacy-flagged task must NEVER route to a net!=none tier.
_contract_ref: Mirrors hybrid_dispatch.router.Router.decide rule order: (1) privacy -> never a net tier; (2) capability-miss -> escalate to the cheapest tier that satisfies; (3) difficulty within a tier's max_difficulty -> that tier; (4) cost tiebreak -> cheapest capable tier. Extended from the router's two lanes (local|frontier) to three by adding 'edge' below 'local'; the upstream router ships local|frontier only (see DEP note in cases.yaml / PROVENANCE.md).
